base.title: How to reduce hallucinations and improve source relevance in a RAG pipeline?
base.excerpt: I have built a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain, a vector database, and an open-source LLM. While it works reasonably well, the model often hallucinates answers or cites sources that are only tangentially related to the user's query. My chunking strategy is set to a chunk size of 1000 tokens, which seems to be the sweet spot for the model.
